lock_of_the_week: Caminero Crush (-475) over Duran Duran — Lock of da week, cher, dis one is locked up tighter dan a tupperware full of mawmaw's chicken salad. Caminero just had dey best week of da year — 48 runs, 11 bombs, 13 SV+H — and now dey gettin' Duran Duran, a team dat’s LAST in K/BB and OBP and ranked 11th in K, QS, and SLG. Duran gon' steal SV+H and SB and den Caminero gon' take da other twelve, I gar-on-tee, that's true.

matchups.blurb: Mais sha, dis is da classic offense vs pitching matchup, like bringin' a plate of bread pudding to a gumbo cook-off — dey’s both good but dey ain't da same thing, that's true. Universal Basic Civale is FIRST in R, FIRST in HR, FIRST in RBI, and FIRST in SLG — dat’s Judge and Soto and Harper and Freeman hittin' bombs like it's da Fourth of July on da lakefront, ooo wee. Polar Express just had da BEST pitching week of any team in Week 4 (2.83 ERA, 0.99 WHIP, 6 QS) — sha, dat's impressive like catchin' a twelve-pound redfish on light tackle, I gar-on-tee. But Polar’s offense is LAST in R, LAST in RBI, and eleventh in SB, which means dey ain't scorin' enough to keep up. UBC got dey own pitching problems (last in K, tied last in W), but dey gon' win da four hitting cats by a mile plus steal a couple pitching cats on volume. Polar gets ERA, WHIP, and maybe QS. Pick: UBC by way of a fully-dressed muffuletta at Central Grocery, that's true.

WOOO DOGGY, mon ami, dis here is a pitcher’s duel like none other — two of da BEST staffs in da league goin' head-to-head like two tantes arguin' over who makes da better file gumbo, that's true. Nine Spoons is FIRST in ERA and FIRST in WHIP on da season, dey pitching is tighter dan da buttons on ya grand-pére's Sunday suit, I gar-on-tee. But here's da thing — Spoons just had dey WORST offensive week of da year (.198 AVG, 7 HR, 27 RBI) and da bats ain’t gon’ magically wake up just 'cause da schedule says so, no. Burns is da #1 team overall, FIRST in AVG, OBP, QS, and K/BB — sha, dat's four first-place cats and a rotation of Sale, Kirby, Woo, Imanaga, and Nola dat's scarier dan a swamp tour at midnight, that's true. Spoons might steal ERA, WHIP, and K/BB 'cause da pitching is legit, plus SB maybe, but Burns gon’ win all da hitting cats and probably wins, QS, and K too. Pick: Burns by way of a pot of chicken and sausage gumbo wit' extra cayenne, mais oui, that’s true.

OOO WEEEEE, cher, dis one gon’ be ugly, like da day after a Mardi Gras parade when da streets is covered in beads and broken plastic cups, that's true. Caminero just put up one of da best all-around weeks of da season (48 R, 11 HR, 38 RBI, 6 W, 80 K, 4.01 ERA, 1.19 WHIP, 13 SV+H) and da model got dem at #2 overall — sha, dat's a balanced attack like a proper seafood boil wit' crawfish AND shrimp AND crabs AND corn AND potatoes, mon ami. Duran Duran got exactly TWO strengths (SV+H and SB) and dey ranked LAST in K/BB, LAST in OBP, and eleventh in K, QS, and SLG — dat's a lot of last places for one team, I gar-on-tee. Yeah Duran gon’ steal SV+H 'cause dat’s dey thing and maybe SB if dey get lucky, but Caminero gon’ take everything else and den some. Lock of da week, cher, save ya money and go buy ya nephew some crawfish instead. Pick: Caminero by way of a whole hog boucherie wit' nothin' left but bones, that's true.

team_a.name: Camp of the New Orleans Saints

matchups.blurb: Mais cher, dis matchup is da hottest team in da league against da most LOPSIDED team in da league, and dat’s a recipe for somethin’ interestin’, that’s true. Camp of da New Orleans Saints is ridin’ a +28 heat score — da biggest in either direction — after puttin’ up 45 R, 13 HR, 46 RBI, 5 W, 5 QS, 8 SV+H last week, sha, dey bats finally woke up like dey been holdin’ onto dat boudin for a week and now dey ready to eat, ooo wee. Yandy got elite bats too (second in R, HR, AVG) but dey pitching is STRAIGHT-UP BAD — last in ERA, last in WHIP, last in QS — so dey gon’ lose four pitching cats automatic, that's true. Camp ain’t great at pitching neither (10th in W, 11th in SV+H) but dey Week 4 pitching was a lot cleaner (4.44 ERA, 5 QS, 8 SV+H) dan Yandy’s (5.15 ERA, 4 QS, 2 SV+H). Dis gon’ come down to who wins da hitting cats — and Camp got da recent hot streak and da edge in RBI and SLG. Pick: Camp by a slim margin, like winnin’ da fais do-do dance contest by half a step, c’est bon, that’s true.

Sha, dis here is another offense vs pitching affair, but PO’ BOYD got somethin’ goin’ dat ShawSwank just don’t — dey got BOTH sides workin’ at least halfway, that’s true. PO’ BOYD just exploded for 18 HR and .532 SLG in Week 4, dat’s da best power week any team has posted all year, I gar-on-tee — Yordan and Schwarber hittin’ like it’s da home run derby at Zephyr Field, that's true. ShawSwank is ALWAYS gon’ win K (dey first in da league), QS (second), and W (second) — dat pitching staff is relentless like mosquitoes in da Atchafalaya, mon ami — but da offense is last in AVG, last in SLG, and eleventh in RBI, which is a lot of ground to give up. PO’ BOYD pitching in Week 4 was rough (4.58 ERA, 1.36 WHIP) so ShawSwank gon’ steal some rate cats, but da offensive gap is just too wide. Pick: PO’ BOYD by way of an oyster po’boy wit' hot sauce, fully dressed, that’s true.

Mais oui, dis one is a WRECK waitin’ to happen for Balls to the Wall, that’s true. Balls just had da worst week of any team in Week 4 (26 R, 4 HR, .235 AVG, 1 W, 5.78 ERA, 1 SV+H) and dey heat score is -29, da coldest in da league. Dat’s what we call around here ‘a team in free fall,’ sha — like a pirogue wit’ a hole in it driftin’ towards da Gulf, I gar-on-tee. CLAUDE just had dey BEST pitching week (2.80 ERA, 1.00 WHIP, 80 K, 3.81 K/BB) and dey got plenty of speed (Ohtani, Acuña, Tatis, Elly — 2nd in SB, 4th in R). Balls got nuttin’ to hang dey hat on right now — dey highest-ranked cat is FIFTH place (W and K), and dey ranked 10th or worse in six categories, ooo wee. CLAUDE gon’ run away wit’ dis one. Balls might steal ERA or WHIP on luck, but even dat’s a stretch. Pick: CLAUDE 4 COMMISH by way of a whole ribeye from Liuzza’s wit’ grilled onions, that’s true.
